title: Impeller
objectives: Explain why Impeller replaced Skia's runtime shader compilation
Describe how Impeller avoids shader jank

sections.markdown: Impeller is Flutter's modern rendering engine, built to replace the Skia-based renderer. Its main goal: eliminate 'shader compilation jank' by precompiling shaders at build time instead of at runtime.

The old problem: with Skia, shaders were compiled the first time an effect appeared, causing a one-time stutter (jank) on first run of an animation. Impeller precompiles a fixed set of shaders ahead of time, so first-run animations are smooth.
Impeller is the default on iOS and increasingly on Android (Vulkan). It's tuned specifically for Flutter's drawing patterns rather than being a general-purpose 2D library.
If you ever saw first-run animation stutters, Impeller is the fix. For deep custom effects, test on Impeller specifically, since some Skia-era shader tricks behave differently.

flashcards.back: Shader compilation jank — the first-run stutter when Skia compiled shaders at runtime.
It precompiles shaders at build time instead of on first use.
Metal on iOS and Vulkan on Android.

quiz.explain: Precompiled shaders remove first-run animation stutter.
Ahead-of-time shader compilation is the key idea.
Impeller uses Vulkan on Android and Metal on iOS.

challenges.text: Explain shader jank and how Impeller addresses it in your own words.
Toggle Impeller on/off and compare first-run animation smoothness.
Profile a complex animation on Impeller vs the legacy renderer.
Investigate a custom shader/effect that renders differently on Impeller and adapt it.

interviewQuestions: What is Impeller and why was it created?
How does Impeller differ from Skia?
Which graphics backends does Impeller use?
